title: Anisopappus boinensis
intro: Anisopappus boinensis is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly referred to as a shrub or herb. Research has identified several health benefits associated with Anisopappus boinensis, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Anisopappus boinensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Anisopappus boinensis is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, coughs, and digestive issues, as well as to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Anisopappus boinensis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Inuleae. As a member of the Inuleae tribe, Anisopappus boinensis is closely related to other species in the same group. The genus Anisopappus is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Anisopappus boinensis is native to Madagascar, where it grows in a variety of habitats.
